rationale: Wrapped det.rs in verus! with required types. The struct StrictlyOrderedVec uses `v: Seq<K>` and view(self) = self.v so view-equality directly yields struct equality (Verus has no Vec ext_equal axiom; Seq has built-in extensional equality). The proof block does a case-split: if all postcondition conjuncts hold, derive r1==r2 and post1@==post2@ by transitivity, then post1.v==post2.v, then post1==post2 by struct extensionality.
